name: TAM Layering: Tesnse Shifted
intuition: This feature is present when the gram overtly encodes a tense or mood specification other than the simple present perfect. It distinguishes the canonical present perfect ('have/has' + past participle) from past, future, modal, or progressive perfect forms.
set_to_1_if: The auxiliary is morphologically past (e.g., 'had known', 'had been sleeping').
The gram includes future marking (e.g., 'will have arrived', 'will have been working').
The gram includes a modal auxiliary preceding 'have' (e.g., 'would have gone', 'could have seen', 'must have done').

Set to 1 only if the gram contains 'been' followed immediately by a present participle ('-ing' form), as in 'has been working' or 'have been reading'. 'Been' followed by a past participle does not count; those are passive constructions, not progressive ones.
set_to_0_if: The gram is formed with present-tense 'have' or 'has' followed by a past participle, with no additional modal, future, or progressive marking.

Do not assign 1 to passive forms in the simple present, such as 'has been broken' or 'have been eaten'. These are simple present passives, not tense-shifted forms, because 'been' is followed by a past participle rather than an '-ing' form.
examples_positive: John has been working a lot. (Progressive -> 1)
examples_negative: I have known him. (Simple Present Perfect -> 0)
The seeds have been planted. (Simple present passtive -> 0)
She has been here before. (Present perfect of 'be'; no progressive -> 0)
